channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says 2 Chinese were caught fighting alongside Russia
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The Ukrainian military has captured two Chinese men fighting alongside the Russian army in the eastern Donetsk region and has information that “significantly more” are with Russian forces, Ukraine’s President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump signs executive orders to boost coal, a reliable but polluting energy source
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday signed a series of executive orders aimed at boosting the struggling coal industry, a reliable but polluting energy source that’s long been in decline.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kentucky watches for surging rivers to recede so widespread cleanup can begin
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — After days of deluges overfilled rivers to near-record levels across Kentucky, residents were anxious Tuesday to return to their flooded homes and assess what's salvageable, even as stubbornly high waters kept some of them waiting even longer.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, KRISTIN M. HALL and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Human remains found in 1973 identified as missing Kansas teen
item.description: Human remains found near Garnett, Kansas, in 1973 have been positively identified more than 50 years later.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, WGN 9

item.title: New Mexico governor mobilizes National Guard to tackle crime emergency in Albuquerque
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham on Tuesday declared a state of emergency in New Mexico’s largest city, saying that a significant increase in crime in Albuquerque warrants the help of the New Mexico National Guard.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Boyfriend of missing woman detained by ICE amid investigation into her disappearance: police
item.description: Maryland investigators searching for clues into the disappearance of a mother of two discovered her boyfriend was allegedly in the U.S. illegally and ICE agents arrested him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Keystone oil pipeline shutdown could quickly lead to higher gasoline prices
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — The nearly 2,700-mile Keystone oil pipeline was shut down Tuesday morning after it ruptured in North Dakota, halting the flow of millions of gallons of crude oil from Canada to refineries in the U.S. and potentially leading to higher gasoline prices.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA and SARAH RAZA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ivy League anti-Israel ringleader Mahmoud Khalil denies allegations as judge awaits deportation evidence
item.description: Mahmoud Khalil, the leader of the pro-Palestinian protests at Columbia University, denied all allegations against him during a court hearing as he faces deportation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump touts Supreme Court deportation ruling as a major victory, but legal fight is far from over
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is touting a Supreme Court ruling allowing it to resume deportations under the Alien Enemies Act as a major victory, but the immigration fight is far from over.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Keystone pipeline shut down after North Dakota rupture
item.description: The affected segment has been isolated, and operations and containment resources have been mobilized to site,” the company said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Watch the world’s largest iceberg run aground near remote island in South Atlantic Ocean
item.description: Newly released satellite footage shows the world’s largest iceberg running aground near a remote island in the South Atlantic Ocean last month.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Judge tells government to provide evidence, or case against Columbia student Mahmoud Khalil is over
item.description: An immigration judge in Louisiana says she will decide later this week whether the government can continue to detain Mahmoud Khalil, the Columbia University activist facing deportation for his role in pro-Palestinian campus protests.
item.dc:creator: Sara Cline And Jake Offenhartz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration cancels $188M in grants New York City was using to shelter migrants
item.description: The Trump administration canceled $188 million in federal grants meant to reimburse New York City for sheltering migrants, saying the money was being spent to support illegal immigration and leading the city's mayor to vow to fight the clawback.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Legal status revoked for 985,000 migrants who entered US under Biden-era CBP One app
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is terminating parole protections for people who entered the country through the CBP One app, revoking legal status for nearly a million migrants who came to the U.S. during the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Fraternity brothers help reunite missing dog with owners after nearly 5 years
item.description: A dog missing for five years had a sweet reunion with its owner thanks to the help of a fraternity at The University of Southern Mississippi.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Last chance to claim $1,400 stimulus check from 2021 nears
item.description: Taxpayers who haven’t filed their 2021 tax returns may still be eligible to receive one (or more) stimulus payments sent during the height of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Photos: 'Jackson Magnolia' removed at White House, replaced with descendant sapling
item.description: The newest greenery added to the White House grounds came the day after one of the southern magnolia trees, which was said to have been planted by Jackson in the 19th century, was removed due to "serious safety concerns.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: US expected a big travel year, but overseas visitors
item.description: Olja Ivanic looked forward to welcoming some cousins from Sweden to her Denver home in June. Ivanic and the four travelers were planning to go hiking in Colorado and then visit Los Angeles and San Francisco.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Future uncertain for Duke basketball star due to new Trump policy
item.description: A new visa policy announced by the Trump administration last weekend could impact South Sudanese passport holders, including Duke basketball phenom Khaman Maluach.
item.dc:creator: Keaton Eberly, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Small plane skids off runway at Oregon airport, ends up in bay
item.description: A small private plane attempting to land at an Oregon airport skidded off the runway and into a body of water, leaving 5 people injured.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Foran, Fox 5

item.title: Florida teens beat up mall employee dressed as Easter Bunny: Police
item.description: A trio of teenagers was accused of attacking a Florida mall employee in an Easter Bunny costume on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: Stephen A. Smith 'leaving all doors open' for presidential bid
item.description: Smith has a longstanding history of weighing in on politics, but has been particularly critical of the Democratic Party in the wake of President Trump’s success in the 2024 election, pushing for it to refocus on top issues for voters in last year’s election cycle including immigration and inflation.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: Former Sen. Joe Manchin is working on a memoir. 'Dead Center' is scheduled for September
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former Sen. Joe Manchin has a memoir coming out in September, what the self-styled maverick from West Virginia calls his “declaration of independence from the extremes on both sides.” St.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: RFK Jr. plans to tell CDC to stop recommending fluoride in drinking water
item.description: The addition of low levels of fluoride to drinking water was long considered one of the greatest public health achievements of the last century.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: RFK Jr. says he plans to tell CDC to stop recommending fluoride in drinking water
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (AP) — U.S. Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. on Monday said he plans to tell the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention to stop recommending fluoridation in communities nationwide.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH SCHOENBAUM and MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Epic Universe opens a portal to the future of theme parks
item.description: As Universal Orlando Resort gears up for the highly anticipated May 22 opening of Epic Universe, it’s clear the resort is entering a new era - one that could redefine what a theme park can offer families.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, Fox 5

item.title: Chief Justice Roberts pauses order to return Maryland dad mistakenly deported to El Salvador
item.description: Chief Justice Roberts agreed to pause a deadline for the Trump administration to return a Maryland man mistakenly deported to El Salvador.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: US stocks dip after careening through a manic day following Trump's latest tariff threat
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. stocks careened through a manic Monday after President Donald Trump threatened to crank his tariffs higher, despite a stunning display showing how dearly Wall Street wants him to do the opposite.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, ELAINE KURTENBACH and DAVID McHUGH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Fan-favorite Krispy Kreme item returning for Easter
item.description: Krispy Kreme is celebrating Easter with an all-new doughnut collection, which includes a fan-favorite item. Starting Monday, fans can enjoy the all-new Hoppy Easter Collection, featuring three festive doughnuts and the strawberry doughnut.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Seven GOP senators sign onto bill to check Trump's trade authority
item.description: Seven Republican senators have signed onto a bipartisan bill that would require Congress to approve President Trump’s steep tariffs on trading partners.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Harriet Tubman reference removed from National Parks' Underground Railroad web page
item.description: The National Parks Service (NPS) has removed a reference to abolitionist Harriet Tubman from its webpage dedicated to the Underground Railroad.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, ABC 4

item.title: Trump honors Dodgers' 2024 World Series championship team
item.description: The 2024 World Series Champions, the Los Angeles Dodgers, were honored by President Donald Trump at the White House on Monday before opening a series with the Washington Nationals.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KRON4

item.title: Miami’s ‘Little Venezuela’ fears Trump's moves against migration
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — Wilmer Escaray left Venezuela in 2007 and enrolled at Miami Dade College, opening his first restaurant six years later.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US stocks dip after careening through a manic day following Trump's latest tariff threat
item.description: U.S. stocks swung wildly in a manic Monday after President Donald Trump doubled down on his tariffs, despite seeing how much Wall Street wants him to do the opposite.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: RFK Jr. says MMR vaccine 'most effective' way to stop spread of measles after Texas child's death
item.description: Kennedy, who has a history of vaccine skepticism, made the remarks after visiting the family of an unvaccinated 8-year-old girl who died from the disease.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: Trump says he's not backing down, calls tariffs 'medicine' as markets reel
item.description: Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One, Trump said he didn't want global markets to fall, but also that he wasn't concerned about the massive sell-off either.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Researchers developing at-home smell test to detect Alzheimer’s
item.description: A smell test is less invasive and more accessible than other methods like PET scans, lumbar punctures or blood tests.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WFLA, NBC
